commit_scope: Classify Rust kernel fatal crashes, preserve source/request context, and keep serve workers alive after recoverable handler panics

commands: cd form/form-kernel-rust && cargo build --quiet && target/debug/form-kernel-rust serve --port <free-port> --routes <tmp-crash-routes.fk> --workers 1; curl /boom; curl /ok
local_validation.notes: The focused Rust tests prove type-contract fatal classification and trace JSON fields. The full Rust kernel suite passed 51/51 in 141.05s, including the source BML catalog route test. The live CLI proof exits 1 with fatal[type_contract_violation]: as_str: Null and writes fatal_kind, fatal_message, likely_root_cause, avoidance, source_head, and rust_backtrace. The one-worker serve proof closes the crashing /boom connection, writes operation=worker=0 request=GET /boom route=/boom handler=/boom, logs recovered fatal in worker 0, and then serves /ok with X-Form-Router: native-kernel from the same worker pool. The trace writer now falls back from .cache/form-kernel-rust to the OS temp trace directory if process-global cwd motion makes the primary cache path unavailable. Hostinger form deploy path proof passed with BML GET/read ingress coverage: 52 routes.

ci_validation.notes: PR CI has not run yet for this branch.

commands: Hostinger Auto Deploy on main after merge
deploy_validation.notes: Deploy proof should confirm the kernel-router still starts and public canaries remain healthy after fatal diagnostics hardening.

phase_gate.reason: Local focused validation passed; PR CI and deploy proof remain pending.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Recoverable Rust kernel handler panics should produce classified fatal diagnostics with source/request operation context and should not silently remove a serve worker from the pool.
test_flows: Trigger a null-to-string type-contract panic through _json_get and str_concat; inspect the classified trace.
Trigger a native route panic on a one-worker serve process, then verify another native route still responds.
